title: Israel Innovation Authority Launches NIS 100M ($33M USD) Call to Build National Quantum R&D Infrastructure - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Israel's Innovation Authority is deploying NIS 100M ($33M) to build a centralized national quantum R&D testbed that must integrate at least three distinct qubit architectures, betting on multi-modality because no single physical platform has won yet.

insights.claim: Israel is committing NIS 100 million (~$33M USD) via the Israel Innovation Authority to stand up a single centralized national quantum computing R&D hub serving both industry and academia.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A defined government anchor budget signals a fundable procurement opportunity for QPU vendors and integrators seeking a national reference customer.
insights.quote: The Israel Innovation Authority (IIA) has announced a NIS 100 million ($33 million USD) call for proposals to establish a centralized national quantum computing research and development infrastructure.
insights.locator: The Israel Innovation Authority (IIA) has announced

insights.claim: The tender explicitly requires the operator to integrate at least three distinct quantum processing technologies into a unified framework, because no single qubit architecture has established dominance.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A hard multi-modality requirement de-risks single-architecture bets and favors vendors and cloud-access layers that are hardware-agnostic.
insights.quote: the IIA mandate explicitly requires the infrastructure operator to integrate at least three distinct quantum processing technologies into a unified framework
insights.locator: Because no single physical qubit architecture

insights.claim: The infrastructure is designed to let Israeli developers test, benchmark, and compare algorithm performance across different physical backends simultaneously rather than committing to one platform.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Cross-backend benchmarking becomes a decision input for which architectures win specific industrial use cases, an early tell on modality winners.
insights.locator: This multi-modality requirement will allow Israeli developers

insights.claim: The contract obligates the winning operator to begin delivering functional R&D services, including cloud-based QPU access and hardware testing, within 12 months of approval.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A 12-month service milestone favors bidders with already-mature cloud QPU stacks over greenfield builders, narrowing the credible bidder pool.
insights.quote: The operator must begin delivering functional R&D services—including cloud-based QPU access, hardware testing, and algorithmic evaluation—within one year of approval.

insights.claim: The full physical and software infrastructure must be built out and operational in Israel within 18 months of approval.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: An aggressive 18-month buildout window sets a near-term catalyst timeline for tracking Israeli quantum commercialization.

insights.claim: The winning consortium is contractually required to continuously upgrade its hardware backends to match international market advancements, not just deliver a static facility.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A continuous-upgrade clause creates recurring hardware refresh demand, effectively a rolling procurement channel for QPU makers.
insights.quote: the contract requires the winning consortium or industrial entity to continuously upgrade its hardware backends to match international market advancements
insights.locator: To ensure the facility remains at the global technological frontier

insights.claim: Israel is explicitly steering the quantum testbed toward defense, financial services, and deep-tech commercialization rather than pure academic research.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Defense and finance as named end-markets signal where the first Israeli quantum revenue and use-case validation will concentrate.
insights.quote: accelerate the commercialization of quantum applications across defense, financial services, and deep-tech sectors
insights.locator: By establishing a centralized, multi-platform testbed

insights.claim: The mandate bundles workforce development, including direct technology transfer to local high-tech startups, into the infrastructure operator's obligations.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Embedded tech-transfer requirements mean the operator effectively seeds a downstream startup pipeline, a long-tail ecosystem play.
